scene_summary: A miniature woodland/native-garden installation shows a large painted wooden plant wall, a seed envelope rack, a clay ground-zone map, engraved restoration stones, a water basin, a rolled garden scroll, brass plant markers, and botanical ledger/specimen pages arranged as visible interaction surfaces.
usable_surfaces: painted Native Plants of Southeast Michigan wall
cedar rack of local seed envelopes
Detroit Land Lab engraved stone cluster
rolled Wild Ones garden design scroll

detected_objects.label: Native Plants of Southeast Michigan Wall
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Large weathered wooden wall with the visible title 'Native Plants of Southeast Michigan' and illustrated plant silhouettes with species labels.

detected_objects.label: Local Seed Envelope Rack
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Left-side wooden rack labeled 'LOCAL SEED SAVED & SHARED' holding multiple visible paper seed packets.

detected_objects.label: Seed Packet Faces
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Individual tan seed envelopes in the rack show labels such as native bergamot, little bluestem, and blue false indigo.

detected_objects.label: Three-Band Garden Ground Map
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Painted ground map divided into labeled DRY, MESIC, and WET zones with stream-like borders and small stones.

detected_objects.label: Dry Upland Prairie Map Label
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Readable ground text 'DRY' and 'UPLAND PRAIRIE' on the left brown zone of the map.

detected_objects.label: Mesic Savannah Map Label
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Central map text reads 'MESIC' with smaller 'SAVANNAH / OPEN WOODLAND'.

detected_objects.label: Wet Meadow Map Label
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Blue right-hand ground zone contains readable 'WET' and 'WET MEADOW / RIPARIAN' label.

detected_objects.label: Detroit Land Lab Stone Cluster
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Three engraved stones on the right read 'DETROIT LAND LAB NORTHWEST DETROIT', 'LOT 48', and 'SOIL. COMMUNITY. FUTURE.'
